\begin{document}
\begin{center}
{\Huge\bfseries\deva{प्रज्ञापारमिताहृदय}}\\[0.5em]
{\LARGE Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya}\\[0.3em]
{\Large Heart Sūtra}\\[1.5em]
{\large Sanskrit Critical Edition}\\[0.5em]
{\normalsize Based on GRETIL text (following Conze 1967)}\\[2em]
\end{center}

\hrule
\vspace{1em}

\section*{Editorial Note}

Following \textcite{nattier1992}, this edition treats the Sanskrit
text as a back-translation from Chinese. The base text is the GRETIL
digital edition, which follows Conze's critical text.

\vspace{1em}
\newpage

\section*{Text}

\subsection*{Section I: Opening}
\deva{आर्य-अवलोकितेश्वरो बोधिसत्त्वो गम्भीरां प्रज्ञापारमिताचर्यां चरमाणो व्यवलोकयति स्म: पञ्च-स्कन्धास्, तांश्च स्वभाव-शून्यान् पश्यति स्म।}

\linenum{1} Ārya-avalokiteśvaro bodhisattvo gambhīrāṃ prajñāpāramitācaryāṃ caramāṇo vyavalokayati sma: pañca-skandhās, tāṃś ca svabhāva-śūnyān paśyati sma.\footnote{Section I error (Attwood 2015): pañca-skandhās should be pañcaskandhāṃs (accusative). The skandhas are objects of vyavalokayati sma ('examined'), not nominative subjects. Also: vyavalokayati sma uses imperfect tense unusual for sūtra style.}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{The noble Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva, while practicing the profound prajñāpāramitā course, looked down and saw the five aggregates, and he saw them as empty of intrinsic nature.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{The noble Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva, while practicing the profound prajñāpāramitā course, looked down and saw the five aggregates, and he saw them as empty of intrinsic nature.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section II: Form and Emptiness}
\deva{इह शारिपुत्र रूपं शून्यता शून्यतैव रूपम्, रूपान् न पृथक् शून्यता शून्यतायाः न पृथग् रूपम्, यद् रूपं सा शून्यता या शून्यता तद् रूपम्; एवमेव वेदना-संज्ञा-संस्कार-विज्ञानानि।}

\linenum{2} Iha Śāriputra rūpaṃ śūnyatā śūnyataiva rūpam, rūpān na pṛthak śūnyatā śūnyatāyā na pṛthag rūpam, yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpam; evam eva vedanā-saṃjñā-saṃskāra-vijñānāni.\footnote{The phrase 'yad rūpaṃ sā śūnyatā yā śūnyatā tad rūpam' has no Chinese counterpart and is absent from most Conze witnesses. Late interpolation not in Large Sutra parallels (Nattier 1992, Attwood 2017a). Original Aṣṭa reading was 'rūpam eva māyā māyaiva rūpam' (appearance is only illusion).}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Here, Śāriputra, form is emptiness, and emptiness itself is form. Emptiness is not separate from form; form is not separate from emptiness. That which is form is emptiness; that which is emptiness is form. Just so are feeling, perception, formation, and consciousness.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Here, Śāriputra, form is emptiness, and emptiness itself is form. Emptiness is not separate from form; form is not separate from emptiness. That which is form is emptiness; that which is emptiness is form. Just so are feeling, perception, formation, and consciousness.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section III: Characteristics}
\deva{इह शारिपुत्र सर्व-धर्माः शून्यता-लक्षणा, अनुत्पन्ना अनिरुद्धा, अमला अविमला, अनूना अपरिपूर्णाः।}

\linenum{3} Iha Śāriputra sarva-dharmāḥ śūnyatā-lakṣaṇā, anutpannā aniruddhā, amalā avimalā, anūnā aparipūrṇāḥ.\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Here, Śāriputra, all dharmas are marked by emptiness: not arisen, not ceased, not defiled, not undefiled, not deficient, not complete.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Here, Śāriputra, all dharmas are marked by emptiness: not arisen, not ceased, not defiled, not undefiled, not deficient, not complete.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section IV: Negations: Skandhas}
\deva{तस्माच् छारिपुत्र शून्यतायां न रूपं न वेदना न संज्ञा न संस्काराः न विज्ञानम्।}

\linenum{4} Tasmāc Chāriputra śūnyatāyāṃ na rūpaṃ na vedanā na saṃjñā na saṃskārāḥ na vijñānam.\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Therefore, Śāriputra, in emptiness there is no form, no feeling, no perception, no formations, no consciousness.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Therefore, Śāriputra, in emptiness there is no form, no feeling, no perception, no formations, no consciousness.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section V: Negations: Āyatanas}
\deva{न चक्षुः-श्रोत्र-घ्राण-जिह्वा-काय-मनांसि। न रूप-शब्द-गन्ध-रस-स्प्रष्टव्य-धर्माः।}

\linenum{5} Na cakṣuḥ-śrotra-ghrāṇa-jihvā-kāya-manāṃsi. Na rūpa-śabda-gandha-rasa-spraṣṭavya-dharmāḥ.\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{No eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, or mind. No form, sound, smell, taste, tangible, or mental object.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{No eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, or mind. No form, sound, smell, taste, tangible, or mental object.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section VI: Negations: Dhātus}
\deva{न चक्षुर्-धातुर् यावन् न मनोविज्ञान-धातुः।}

\linenum{6} Na cakṣur-dhātur yāvan na manovijñāna-dhātuḥ.\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{No eye-element, up to and including no mind-consciousness element.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{No eye-element, up to and including no mind-consciousness element.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section VII: Negations: Pratītyasamutpāda}
\deva{न-अविद्या न-अविद्या-क्षयो यावन् न जरा-मरणं न जरा-मरण-क्षयो।}

\linenum{7} Na-avidyā na-avidyā-kṣayo yāvan na jarā-maraṇaṃ na jarā-maraṇa-kṣayo.\footnote{Uses kṣaya (destruction) instead of standard nirodha (cessation) - evidence of back-translation (Nattier 1992)}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{No ignorance, no destruction of ignorance, up to and including no old age and death, no destruction of old age and death.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{No ignorance, no destruction of ignorance, up to and including no old age and death, no destruction of old age and death.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section VIII: Negations: Noble Truths}
\deva{न दुःख-समुदय-निरोध-मार्गा। न ज्ञानं, न प्राप्तिर् न-अप्राप्तिः।}

\linenum{8} Na duḥkha-samudaya-nirodha-mārgā. Na jñānaṃ, na prāptir na-aprāptiḥ.\footnote{na-aprāptiḥ is a late interpolation not found in any Large Sutra version (Huifeng 2014). Should be 'na prāptir nābhisamayaḥ' (no attainment, no realization). The pair prāpti/abhisamaya refers to mārga/phala (path and fruition). Kumārajīva's T223 error was copied into the Heart Sutra (Attwood 2020a).}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{No suffering, origination, cessation, or path. No knowledge, no attainment, and no non-attainment.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{No suffering, origination, cessation, or path. No knowledge, no attainment, and no non-attainment.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section IX: Bodhisattva Result}
\deva{तस्माच् छारिपुत्र अप्राप्तित्वाद् बोधिसत्त्वस्य प्रज्ञापारमितामाश्रित्य विहरत्यचित्तावरणः। चित्तावरण-नास्तित्वाद् अत्रस्त्रो विपर्यास-अतिक्रान्तो निष्ठा-निर्वाण-प्राप्तः।}

\linenum{9} Tasmāc Chāriputra aprāptitvād bodhisattvasya prajñāpāramitām āśritya viharaty acittāvaraṇaḥ. Cittāvaraṇa-nāstitvād atrastro viparyāsa-atikrānto niṣṭhā-nirvāṇa-prāptaḥ.\footnote{Section VI error (Attwood 2018a, 2020a): Conze's wrongly placed full stop creates a sentence fragment. niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ is a bahuvrīhi compound (masculine adjective) meaning 'one whose nirvāṇa is complete' - it describes the bodhisattva, not a separate neuter noun. Corrected reading removes the period: 'viharaty acittāvaraṇaś cittāvaraṇanāstitvād atrasto viparyāsātikrānto niṣṭhānirvāṇaḥ'.}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Therefore, Śāriputra, because of non-attainment, the bodhisattva, relying on prajñāpāramitā, dwells with mind unobstructed. Because of the absence of mental obstruction, he is unafraid, has gone beyond error, and has attained ultimate nirvāṇa.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Therefore, Śāriputra, because of non-attainment, the bodhisattva, relying on prajñāpāramitā, dwells with mind unobstructed. Because of the absence of mental obstruction, he is unafraid, has gone beyond error, and has attained ultimate nirvāṇa.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section X: Buddha Result}
\deva{त्र्यध्व-व्यवस्थिताः सर्व-बुद्धाः प्रज्ञापारमितामाश्रित्यानुत्तरां सम्यक्-संबोधिमभिसंबुद्धाः।}

\linenum{10} Tryadhva-vyavasthitāḥ sarva-buddhāḥ prajñāpāramitām āśrityānuttarāṃ samyak-saṃbodhim abhisaṃbuddhāḥ.\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{All buddhas abiding in the three times, relying on prajñāpāramitā, have fully awakened to unsurpassed, perfect, complete enlightenment.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{All buddhas abiding in the three times, relying on prajñāpāramitā, have fully awakened to unsurpassed, perfect, complete enlightenment.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section XI: Mantra Praise}
\deva{तस्माज् ज्ञातव्यम्: प्रज्ञापारमिता महा-मन्त्रो महा-विद्या-मन्त्रो ऽनुत्तर-मन्त्रो ऽसमसम-मन्त्रः, सर्व-दुःख-प्रशमनः, सत्यम् अमिथ्यत्वात्।}

\linenum{11} Tasmāj jñātavyam: prajñāpāramitā mahā-mantro mahā-vidyā-mantro 'nuttara-mantro 'samasama-mantraḥ, sarva-duḥkha-praśamanaḥ, satyam amithyatvāt.\footnote{Epithets section (Attwood 2017b): Standard Prajñāpāramitā has three epithets (mahā-, anuttara-, asamasama-) representing vidyā, not mantra. Gilgit Pañc has 'mahāvidyeyaṃ...anuttareyaṃ vidyā...asamasameyaṃ vidyā'. Chinese míngzhòu 明咒 represents vidyā. The fourth epithet shénzhòu 神咒 may be a synonym for míngzhòu.}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Therefore it should be known: prajñāpāramitā is the great mantra, the mantra of great knowledge, the unsurpassed mantra, the mantra equal to the unequalled, which allays all suffering — true, because not false.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Therefore it should be known: prajñāpāramitā is the great mantra, the mantra of great knowledge, the unsurpassed mantra, the mantra equal to the unequalled, which allays all suffering — true, because not false.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section XII: Mantra}
\deva{प्रज्ञापारमितायामुक्तो मन्त्रः। तद्यथा: ॐ गते गते पारगते पारसंगते बोधि स्वाहा।}

\linenum{12} Prajñāpāramitāyām ukto mantraḥ. Tadyathā: Oṃ gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.\footnote{Oṃ before 'gate gate' is absent from most Sanskrit manuscripts including the Hōryūji manuscript, and from all Chinese versions except T.257 (11th century Dānapāla translation). Late tantric interpolation (Attwood 2024). Conze (1975) omits oṃ from his translation.}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{The mantra is proclaimed in the prajñāpāramitā. It runs thus: Oṃ gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{The mantra is proclaimed in the prajñāpāramitā. It runs thus: Oṃ gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.}

\vspace{1em}

\end{document}
